data.text: act or an instance of contradicting
examples: the defendant's contradiction of the plaintiff's accusations
… both parts of a contradiction cannot possibly be true …
a round square is a contradiction in terms

data.text: a proposition, statement, or phrase that asserts or implies both the truth and falsity of something
examples: the defendant's contradiction of the plaintiff's accusations
… both parts of a contradiction cannot possibly be true …
a round square is a contradiction in terms

data.text: a statement or phrase whose parts contradict each other
examples: the defendant's contradiction of the plaintiff's accusations
… both parts of a contradiction cannot possibly be true …
a round square is a contradiction in terms

enrichment.etymology: Middle English contradiccioun "objection, opposition, contradiction in logic" borrowed from Anglo-French & Latin; Anglo-French contradiction, borrowed from Latin contrādictiōn-, contrādictiō "act of speaking in opposition, counterargument" (Late Latin also "contradiction in logic," after Greek antíphasis), from contrādic-, variant stem of contrādīcere "to speak against, object to" + -tiōn-, -tiō, suffix of verbal action
